title: How to Boost SEO Rankings in GoHighLevel — Complete Walkthrough
description: Learn how to boost SEO rankings in GoHighLevel with keyword research, site audits, backlink analysis, and content optimization. Complete walkthrough inside.
html_content:
Running an agency means juggling countless tools—analytics platforms, SEO checkers, content optimizers, reporting dashboards. What if you didn't have to?

GoHighLevel's integrated SEO feature, powered by Search Atlas, puts enterprise-level SEO capabilities directly into your CRM. You can run keyword research, audit websites, analyze backlinks, optimize content, and generate reports—all without leaving the platform your clients are already using.

In this complete walkthrough, I'll show you exactly how to set up GoHighLevel's SEO tools, leverage its most powerful features, and use it to drive real ranking improvements for your clients. Whether you're a solo freelancer or managing multiple agency accounts, this guide will save you time, money, and complexity. Ready to simplify your stack? Start your free 30-day trial here—that's double the standard trial period.

📋 In This Guide

  • How to Set Up GoHighLevel's SEO Feature
  • Navigating the SEO Dashboard
  • Keyword Research and Analysis
  • Website Audits and Technical SEO
  • Backlink Analysis and Competition Research
  • Content Optimization for Rankings
  • SEO Reporting and Tracking Progress
  • Pricing, Quotas, and Agency Billing

How to Set Up GoHighLevel's SEO Feature

Getting started with GoHighLevel's SEO tool takes just a few minutes. First, log into your GoHighLevel account and navigate to the main dashboard. From there, you'll find the SEO section in the left sidebar—it may be labeled "SEO" or "Search Atlas" depending on your account version.

Click into the SEO module and you'll be prompted to add your first website. Enter your client's domain, and GoHighLevel will immediately begin scanning the site for technical issues, on-page optimization gaps, and ranking opportunities. The platform connects directly to Google Search Console and Google Analytics (if you authorize it) to pull real performance data.

For agencies managing multiple client sites, you can add each client's website as a separate project. This keeps your workspace organized and ensures reporting stays siloed by account. Each site gets its own dashboard, keyword tracking, audit history, and content recommendations.

💡 Pro Tip

Authorize your Google Search Console account during setup. This gives GoHighLevel access to real impression data, click data, and ranking positions—far more accurate than estimates alone.

Navigating the SEO Dashboard

Once your site is added, you'll land on the main SEO dashboard. This is your command center for all optimization work. The dashboard displays your site's overall SEO health score, the number of tracked keywords, current rankings, and priority action items flagged by the system.

The top navigation gives you access to five core sections: Keywords, Site Audit, Backlinks, Content, and Reports. Each section serves a specific purpose in your optimization workflow.

The dashboard also shows your quota usage at a glance. GoHighLevel allocates monthly credits for various actions—keyword scans, backlink checks, content analysis—so you'll always know how many audits or scans you have remaining. This prevents surprises and helps you budget your workflow efficiently across multiple clients.

Keyword Research and Analysis

The Keywords section is where strategy begins. Click into Keywords and you'll see a list of terms your site currently ranks for, sorted by position, search volume, and difficulty. This data comes directly from your Google Search Console integration, so it reflects real performance.

To find new keyword opportunities, use the "Find Keywords" tool. Enter a seed keyword related to your client's business, and GoHighLevel's Search Atlas engine returns hundreds of related terms with search volume, difficulty scores, and cost-per-click data. You can filter by difficulty level (easy wins vs. long-term targets) and save your favorites to a tracking list.

The platform prioritizes keywords by opportunity score—balancing search volume, difficulty, and current ranking position. This helps you focus on terms that are both achievable and valuable, rather than chasing massive volume keywords you can't realistically rank for.

Once you add keywords to your tracking list, GoHighLevel monitors your rankings daily and alerts you to movement. You'll see which keywords are gaining ground and which need extra content support.

Website Audits and Technical SEO

The Site Audit feature crawls your client's entire website and flags technical SEO issues that could be blocking rankings. Run an audit by clicking "Start New Audit" and the crawler gets to work, examining hundreds of on-page and technical factors.

The audit report surfaces issues like broken links, missing meta descriptions, duplicate content, slow page load times, mobile usability problems, and SSL certificate errors. Each issue is ranked by severity—critical issues that hurt rankings appear at the top, while minor recommendations follow.

Beyond identifying problems, GoHighLevel provides specific fix recommendations. For example, if a page is missing an H1 tag, the report tells you exactly what to add and where. This hands-on guidance means you or your client can act immediately rather than needing to hire a developer.

You can schedule audits to run automatically on a weekly or monthly basis, so you're always monitoring for new technical issues. Track audit history over time to prove improvements to clients in your reports.

Backlink Analysis and Competition Research

Backlinks remain one of the strongest ranking signals. GoHighLevel's Backlinks section gives you a complete view of your client's link profile—where their links come from, the authority of those referring domains, and which links might be hurting more than helping.

The backlink report shows referring domains, anchor text used, link authority scores, and when each link was discovered. You can filter by "healthy" vs. "toxic" links to identify any spammy or low-quality links that should be disavowed.

More powerfully, you can analyze your competitors' backlink profiles. See where your top-ranking competitors are getting links, then identify opportunities to pitch those same publications or resource pages. This competitive intelligence accelerates link building strategy—you're not guessing where to pitch, you're following what works.

The platform tracks new backlinks automatically, so you'll be notified when a client earns a new link or when a competitor picks up a major backlink you should know about.

Content Optimization for Rankings

Content optimization in GoHighLevel uses AI-powered analysis to compare your pages against top-ranking competitors. In the Content section, select any page or blog post you want to optimize. The platform analyzes the top 10 ranking results for your target keyword and tells you exactly what's missing from your content.

The optimization report shows recommended word count, heading structure, keywords to include naturally, and topics competitors are covering that you should address. This isn't generic SEO advice—it's specific, competitive analysis based on pages actually ranking above you.

You get a content score (0-100) that improves as you implement recommendations. Many users report ranking improvements within 2-4 weeks of making these optimizations, especially when combined with updated backlinks.

The Content Optimizer also works for meta titles and descriptions, suggesting improvements that will increase click-through rates from search results.

SEO Reporting and Tracking Progress

Agencies live and die by reporting. GoHighLevel's Reports section generates professional, client-ready dashboards that showcase SEO progress. Create monthly reports with just a few clicks, showing keyword rankings, traffic changes, backlinks gained, technical fixes applied, and more.

Reports are fully customizable—white-label them with your agency branding, select which metrics matter most to your clients, and choose between different report formats. You can send reports automatically on a schedule, so clients see progress every month without you lifting a finger.

The dashboard also tracks your own KPIs—organic traffic growth, keyword rankings improvement, conversion data if connected to your analytics—so you always have proof of ROI to share during renewal conversations.

Pricing, Quotas, and Agency Billing

GoHighLevel's SEO pricing is built into the main platform subscription. You don't pay separately for SEO—it's included with your plan. However, each plan tier includes monthly quotas for certain actions like backlink scans, keyword audits, and content analysis.

Starter plans include modest quotas suitable for 1-2 client sites. Pro and Agency plans offer higher quotas that support 10+ active client SEO projects. If you exceed your monthly quota, you can purchase additional credits at transparent per-action rates.

For agencies, the SubAccount feature lets you manage client accounts separately while controlling billing from a master account. Each SubAccount gets its own SEO quota, and you can set different permission levels so clients see only their own data.

This structure means you can offer SEO as a bundled service (included with your CRM package) or as an upsell, depending on your business model. Many agencies find SEO services have excellent margins when delivered through GoHighLevel since there's no third-party tool cost.

Common Questions About GoHighLevel SEO

Can I track rankings for multiple domains in one account?

Yes. GoHighLevel lets you add unlimited websites as separate projects within one account. Each gets its own keyword tracking, audit history, and reporting dashboard.

Does GoHighLevel integrate with Google Search Console?

Absolutely. Authorization is optional but recommended. When connected, you get real impression data, click data, and ranking positions from Google Search Console synced directly into your keyword reports.

How often are keyword rankings updated?

GoHighLevel checks your tracked keywords daily. You'll see ranking positions, changes, and alerts for significant movement updated each morning.

Can clients see their own SEO data?

Yes, through SubAccounts. Set up a SubAccount for each client, and they log in to see only their website's SEO metrics, reports, and recommendations—not your other clients' data.
